generated_comment: I think every couple finds their own rhythm. For my wife and me, we just naturally figured out what each of us is good at. She's amazing with managing the home and the kids' schedules, really diligent. I handle the professional side and the finances. Honestly, it's just more practical and efficient for us this way. No point forcing things that don't come naturally, right? Everyone's happy.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This comment frames a traditional gendered division of labor ('he handles finances, she handles home') as a natural, practical, and mutually agreed-upon choice. It subtly reinforces the belief that women are inherently better at household and childcare management, and men at external/financial tasks, thereby masking underlying sexist expectations as mere efficiency.
original_frame: Motherhood as Basis of Respect, Women Defined by Male Relations
persona_text: You are Suresh. Age 41. Married. A man in Bengaluru. You are posting under your real name on a parenting Facebook group after a thread was started by a childless woman by choice who wrote about feeling judged at family gatherings and asked for advice on how to handle relatives who keep asking when she will have children. You are writing a comment responding to her post and giving your opinion on her situation and whether the relatives asking her are actually wrong and what you think her life is missing that she cannot see yet.
